one_line_definition: Rate of fractional change in length per unit temperature change (K⁻¹ or °C⁻¹)
Cast iron with graphite in a compacted/worm-like morphology between flake (grey) and spheroidal (ductile) forms
Cast iron in which graphite forms as nodules/spheres due to Mg addition
Percentage of graphite present in spheroidal (ISO form VI) or near-spheroidal form; higher = more ductile-like behaviour
Measurement technique for thermal expansion using precise tracking of specimen length change vs. temperature
Ratio of pearlite to ferrite in the matrix of a cast iron or steel; affects strength, hardness, and CTE
Stress at which 0.2% permanent (plastic) strain occurs; used as yield point for non-yielding materials like aluminium
Empirical stress-strain equation ε = σ/E + 0.002(σ/f₀.₂)^n used to describe the continuous yielding of metals
Slow, progressive plastic deformation under sustained stress below yield, especially significant at elevated temperatures
Final stage of creep in which deformation rate increases rapidly, leading to fracture
Interaction between diffusing solute atoms and moving dislocations causing serrations in stress-strain curve and non-monotonic ductility variation with temperature
European structural standard for design of aluminium alloy structures, including fire resistance provisions and reduction factor tables
Stress calculated using instantaneous (current) cross-sectional area: σ_true = F/A_current
Strain calculated as integral of dL/L: ε_true = ln(L/L₀) = ln(1 + e) where e is engineering strain
σ = Kε^n relating true stress to true strain in the plastic region; K is strength coefficient, n is strain hardening exponent
Experimental method using printed grids on specimen surface to measure local strain from photographic images of deformed grid

aliases_seen: elastic modulus, modulus of elasticity, E
one_line_definition: Ratio of normal stress to normal strain in the linear elastic region of the stress-strain curve (GPa)
Negative ratio of transverse strain to axial strain under uniaxial stress; typically 0.25–0.35 for metals
Graphical method to find principal stresses, maximum shear stress, and stress on any plane from a given stress state
Minimum axial compressive load at which a slender column becomes unstable: Pcr = π²EI/Le²
σ = My/I relating bending stress to moment M, distance from neutral axis y, and second moment of area I
Analytical solution for hoop and radial stress in thick-walled cylinders under pressure: σ_θ = A + B/r², σ_r = A − B/r²
Stress component acting circumferentially in a pressurised cylinder; largest at bore in thick-walled cylinders
Assembly method where two parts have a designed dimensional interference; contact pressure develops on assembly, creating residual compressive stress
Yielding occurs when the von Mises equivalent stress √[½((σ₁−σ₂)²+(σ₂−σ₃)²+(σ₃−σ₁)²)] = σ_y
Yielding occurs when maximum shear stress reaches half the uniaxial yield stress: τ_max = σ_y/2
Point through which a transverse load must act to cause bending without twisting of a beam
Ratio of peak local stress to nominal stress at a geometric discontinuity (notch, hole, fillet)
Process of applying internal pressure beyond yield in a thick-walled cylinder to introduce beneficial compressive residual stresses at the bore, increasing fatigue life
Time-dependent reduction in stress under constant strain conditions; related to creep through elastic accommodation
Constitutive equation for steady-state creep: dε/dt = Aσ^n·t^m; parameters A, n, m determined empirically
Equipment for thermomechanical testing with direct electrical resistance heating and servo-hydraulic loading at high strain rates
Phenomenon in which a twisted rod extends axially due to geometric nonlinearity; predicted by nonlinear elasticity
Seven mathematical conditions a constitutive equation must satisfy (frame indifference, causality, determinism, etc.) per Truesdell (1952)
Formulation of continuum mechanics in reference to the original (undeformed) configuration
Formulation of continuum mechanics in reference to the current (deformed) configuration
Material constants relating stress to strain in linear elasticity: λ = Eν/[(1+ν)(1−2ν)], μ = G = E/[2(1+ν)]
Frequency at which a dynamic system oscillates freely without damping; ωn = √(k/m) for SDOF
Ratio of actual damping to critical damping; ζ < 1 underdamped, ζ = 1 critically damped, ζ > 1 overdamped
Decomposition of structural response into orthogonal mode shapes and natural frequencies by solving eigenvalue problem
Complex ratio of output (response) to input (force) as a function of frequency in the frequency domain
Any dimensionally consistent equation with n variables and k fundamental dimensions can be expressed with n−k dimensionless groups
Nine-component (3×3 symmetric) tensor relating traction force per unit area to surface normal; fully describes stress state at a point
Thermodynamic potential for elastic materials; state equations (Hooke's law) derive from σ_ij = ∂ψ/∂ε_ij
Energy released per unit area of crack growth; fracture occurs when G reaches critical value G_c
Localised section in a beam or frame where all fibres have yielded, allowing rotation under constant moment M_p (plastic moment)
Parameter describing the severity of stress field at a crack tip; fracture occurs when K_I = K_IC (fracture toughness)
Fatigue failure criterion relating alternating stress amplitude to mean stress with failure line connecting endurance limit (zero mean) and UTS (zero amplitude)
Deflection at a point equals partial derivative of total strain energy with respect to the force at that point: δ_i = ∂U/∂F_i
For linear elastic structures, the response to combined loads equals the sum of responses to each load acting separately
Model of continuous support where foundation reaction is proportional to local deflection: q = kw (spring constant per unit area)
Aluminium alloy: 0.2% proof stress ~288 MPa at 20°C, good weldability and extrusion; widely used in structural engineering
